title: Nuance healthcare cloud acquisition for 19.7 billion dollars
decisionQuestion: Should Microsoft proceed with the acquisition of Nuance for $19.7 billion?
situation: Microsoft announced a definitive agreement to acquire Nuance for $56.00 per share.
conditions: The merger agreement must be adopted by Nuance stockholders.
Certain governmental and regulatory approvals must be received.
constraints: The transaction is an all-cash deal.
The acquisition is valued at $19.7 billion inclusive of net debt.

options.description: Proceed with the acquisition as agreed.
options.expectedBenefit: Realize value creation through strong execution.

options.description: Re-evaluate the acquisition terms or halt the deal.
options.expectedBenefit: Mitigate risks associated with integration or execution.
options.principalRisk: Missing the opportunity to acquire Nuance.

chosenAction: Proceed with the acquisition as agreed.
rejectedBenefit: Mitigating risks associated with integration or execution.
observations: The deal is an all-cash transaction valued at $19.7 billion.
The agreement implies a 23% premium to Nuance's April 9 closing price.
inferences: Value creation is dependent on terrific execution post-acquisition.
The transaction is subject to various termination and performance risks.

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Microsoft Corp (Nasdaq: MSFT) and Nuance Communications, Inc. (Nasdaq: NUAN) today announced they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Microsoft will acquire Nuance for $56.00 per share, implying a 23% premium to the closing price of Nuance on Friday, April 9, in an all-cash transaction valued at $19.7 billion, inclusive of Nuance’s net debt.
the occurrence of any event, change or other circumstance that could give rise to the termination of the merger agreement
And with all acquisitions, value was ultimately only created with terrific execution, bind aligned team with a shared vision of the future.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Microsoft Corp (Nasdaq: MSFT) and Nuance Communications, Inc. (Nasdaq: NUAN) today announced they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Microsoft will acquire Nuance for $56.00 per share, implying a 23% premium to the closing price of Nuance on Friday, April 9, in an all-cash transaction valued at $19.7 billion, inclusive of Nuance’s net debt.
